rationale: No-spec round (sdlc-light has no spec/story artifact) - acceptance lens skipped, confidence forced to low. Blind + edge-case lenses ran and surfaced 2 real, unambiguous defects in the completed_-prefix fix, both bucketed patch/major: a double-counting risk when both filename variants coexist for the same session, and missing test coverage for resolveSessionPath's fallback/precedence/partial-rename cases. 2 findings deferred as pre-existing (not introduced by this diff): the TOCTOU window between existsSync and the later read, and the lack of path validation on the CLI-supplied sessionId (flagged by the security standard but present since the original single-path join()). 1 finding dismissed as unconfirmed speculation (a hypothetical other caller populating sessionId with a completed_ prefix - not found in the codebase by the edge-case lens's repo exploration). Also noting for the human reviewer: this diff includes one unrelated commit (fix(utils): jwt_token -> external_token) needed to unblock the pre-commit typecheck gate, done with explicit user approval mid-flow and committed separately.

standards_review.notes: All 3 commits use allowed type/scope and subjects under 100 chars, per commitlint.config.cjs and .ai-run/guides/standards/git-workflow.md.
ES module imports keep .js extensions, no `any`, explicit types on the new resolveSessionPath() signature; consistent with surrounding code and .ai-run/guides/standards/code-quality.md.

resolveSessionPath() concatenates the CLI-supplied sessionId into a filesystem path with no validation, per .ai-run/guides/security/security-practices.md's path-validation practice. Pre-existing (the original single join() call had the same gap) and not introduced or worsened by this diff - tracked as a deferred, non-blocking observation, not a new finding.

findings.title: Missing coverage for resolveSessionPath fallback edge cases
findings.problem: No test exercises resolveSessionPath() when neither <id>.json nor completed_<id>.json exists, when both variants coexist for the same id, or when the .json and _metrics.jsonl files are renamed independently (partial rename).
findings.impact: The fallback logic's core guarantees (graceful miss, active-file precedence, independent per-suffix resolution) are unverified; a regression in any of them would ship silently.
findings.recommendation: Add three unit tests to data-loader.test.ts: (1) loadSession returns null when neither filename variant exists, (2) the active <id>.json wins when both <id>.json and completed_<id>.json are present for the same id, (3) resolveSessionPath resolves .json and _metrics.jsonl independently when only one has been renamed to the completed_ prefix.

findings.title: Duplicate sessionId entries when both filename variants exist
findings.problem: loadSessions() iterates every matching .json file and strips the completed_ prefix per-file, but never dedupes by the resulting sessionId. If both <id>.json and completed_<id>.json exist for the same session (e.g. an interrupted/non-atomic hook.ts rename), both filenames now strip to the identical bare UUID, and each loop iteration's loadSession(sessionId) call resolves via resolveSessionPath() to the SAME (active) file - so the same session is loaded and pushed into sessions[] twice.
findings.impact: Aggregated analytics (turns, tool calls, lines changed, cost) for that session would be double-counted in the CLI summary, exported JSON/CSV, and the HTML report - silently skewing totals for exactly the completed-session scenario this fix targets.
findings.recommendation: In the loadSessions() loop, track sessionIds already added (e.g. a Set) and skip a sessionFile whose derived, prefix-stripped sessionId was already loaded earlier in this scan.
